page: Microsoft Azure E96ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. The architecture is arm64, with a cache configuration of 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 128 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data highlights the multi-core capabilities of this architecture. While single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls in the average tier, multi-core execution is highly competitive, ranking in the top tier for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory performance is a major strength, with read and write bandwidth for both small and large blocks scoring in the top tier. Database operations, including Redis SET operations and PassMark database benchmarks, also perform in the top tier. However, LLM inference text generation for small models shows weaker performance, placing in the bottom 25%, though text generation for medium and large models remains strong to top-tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E96ps_v6 offers high resource density and memory capacity per core on an ARM architecture, which typically provides a favorable performance-per-watt and resource-density profile compared to traditional x86 architectures. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs means users only pay for high-capacity memory and compute resources. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E96ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epsv6 family, featuring 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture and running under Microsoft Hyper-V, it is equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM, translating to 7.0 GB of memory per core. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show average single-core results but top-tier multi-core capabilities, particularly in memory bandwidth, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations like Redis SET. While LLM text generation for small models is a relative weak point, the high memory density and multi-core performance make this instance highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications. It is best suited for large databases, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 672 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96ps_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 96 vCPUs and 672 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 virtual machine delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and multi-core performance for enterprise database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
672.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing performance
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis, and software compilation
